source_pr.title: executor, types: copy enum/set instead of refer the underlying data
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #16854  <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
Copy enum/set instead of referring the underlying data in GetMysqlEnum and GetMysqlSet

How it Works:

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Integration test


Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more MEM

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->
- Fix unstable result when select distinct enum column or set column.

symptoms: PR body: fix unstable result when select distinct enum/set column.
failure_signature: PR body: fix unstable result when select distinct enum/set column.
fix_pattern: executor, types: copy enum/set instead of refer the underlying data

evidence: evidence: PR body: fix unstable result when select distinct enum/set column.

evidence: deep-copies Enum/Set values (Copy()) and uses Copy() in firstRow aggregation to avoid referencing underlying data; adds TestIssue16854 to assert stable DISTINCT results (types/enum.go, types/set.go, executor/aggfuncs/func_first_row.go, executor/executor_test.go).
